mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) exhibit a persistent upward trend reflecting consumer demand growth. This trend supports corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS growth rate with forward 20-day returns is less than 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the signal fails to produce positive returns in a majority of periods.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above a threshold signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer spending and corporate profits, thus negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns following UNRATE > 6.5% are positive in more than 60% of instances, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with continued payroll expansion (PAYEMS) indicates a productivity slowdown that squeezes corporate margins and foreshadows negative earnings surprises, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls month-over-month while PAYEMS rises
failure_condition: If forward 20-day returns are positive in over 50% of quarters where INDPRO declines and PAYEMS rises, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is elevated above 2.5%, the Federal Reserve typically responds with rate hikes (FEDFUNDS rising), which tighten financial conditions and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns. In low inflation regimes, rate hikes are less detrimental.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return following this trigger is positive, or if the signal does not produce a negative excess return relative to unconditional average, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO) of more than 0.5% in a single month signals an immediate industrial recession that rapidly reprices earnings expectations downward, causing negative S&P 500 returns within a week.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return following such a decline is positive, or if the signal's accuracy is below 55% over a 3-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects economic expansion that supports corporate profits and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward 60-day returns is below 0.03 over any 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts (HOUST) while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low signals accommodative monetary policy successfully stimulating the housing market, a key demand driver, which positively impacts S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If forward 20-day returns are negative in more than half of triggered instances, or if the average return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a slack labor market (UNRATE above 7%), rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals a robust recovery that boosts consumption and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns. Conversely, in a tight market, rising payrolls would signal overheating.
failure_condition: If forward 20-day returns following this trigger are negative in more than 40% of cases, or if the average return is not significantly greater than zero, the hypothesis is falsified.
